message:
feat(runtime): Apply vtable to all PyValue comparison methods

Extended vtable-based dynamic dispatch to cover all comparison operators,
not just equality. Implements full Python rich comparison protocol with
NotImplemented handling and reflected operations.

PyValue Comparison Methods (object.zig):
- Updated lt(): Try __lt__(other), then other.__gt__(self), with vtable dispatch
- Updated le(): Try __le__(other), then other.__ge__(self), with vtable dispatch
- Updated gt(): Try __gt__(other), then other.__lt__(self), with vtable dispatch
- Updated ge(): Try __ge__(other), then other.__le__(self), with vtable dispatch
- All methods handle NotImplemented returns and fall back to next protocol step

operator.zig Comparison Functions:
- Added rich comparison protocol to lt(), le(), gt(), ge() for class instances
- Check @hasDecl for dunder methods, call with comptime dispatch
- Try primary method, then reflected method, following Python semantics
- Return false if no comparison methods available (prevents native operator use)

Runtime Switches:
- Updated toBoolValue() in bool_ops.zig to handle .type_obj, .object, .not_implemented

Testing Status:
- test_eq.py confirmed working - __eq__ called correctly via vtable
- Full comparison test pending due to unrelated error union type issue
  (operator functions receive *const !A instead of *const A)

Architecture:
The vtable pattern now covers all 6 rich comparison operators, providing
consistent dynamic dispatch for class instances wrapped in PyValue.object.
Each operator follows Python's protocol: try self.method(other), try
other.reflected_method(self), handle NotImplemented, fall back to defaults.

Related: a0cc2cf0 (initial vtable implementation)
